# Market Movers

## For future Claude
Market Movers is a direct SlabMetrics competitor — a multi-tier sports card platform with the strongest community/content moat in the space. Relevant when: assessing the intelligence reports feature (closest incumbent analog to signal analysis), evaluating the community-as-retention-moat strategy, or understanding the $9.99–$49.99/mo pricing ladder.
Key fact: Intelligence Reports (grade/player/variation ratios) is the only incumbent feature that approaches analytical signal generation — but it still only presents ratios, not recommendations.

## TL;DR

Market Movers is a sports card analytics and collection platform with comps, alerts, and Intelligence Reports (grade/variation ratio analysis). 3 paid tiers from $9.99–$49.99/mo. 75,000+ users. Strong community/content flywheel. No buy/hold/sell synthesis. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]

## Core Features

- **Sales history & price charts**: Tens of millions of comps back to 2001. Cards and sealed boxes. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]
- **Collection tracking**: Cards and boxes; stats by sport, year, player, set, grade; P&L tracking. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]
- **Card scanning**: Graded and raw cards; pulls historical sales instantly. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]
- **Deals**: Surfaces cards listed below recent comps across partner marketplaces. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]
- **Price alerts**: Notifications on target-price hits. 5 (Starter), 20 (Premium), unlimited (Unlimited). `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]
- **Market Pulse**: Macro hobby index. Premium and above. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]
- **Intelligence Reports**: Grade ratios, player ratios, variation ratios — designed to find market inefficiencies before the broader hobby. Unlimited tier only. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]

## Data Sources

eBay, Fanatics Collect, Goldin, Heritage Auctions, Pristine Auctions, Cards HQ, MySlabs, [[entities/psa|PSA]], SGC, Beckett, CGC. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]

## Pricing

| Tier | Price/mo | Collection | Alerts | Key extras |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Starter | $9.99 | 25 items | 5 | Comps, charts, trends, deals |
| Premium | $24.99 | 250 items | 20 | + Market Pulse |
| Unlimited | $49.99 | Unlimited | Unlimited | + Intelligence Reports, founder calls |

Annual plans: 17% discount. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]

## Competitive Assessment (vs. SlabMetrics)

**Their advantage**: Community and content flywheel (75k users, monthly livestreams, private calls) is a significant retention moat. Intelligence Reports approaches signal analysis — the most sophisticated analytical feature of any incumbent.

**Their weakness**: Alert-driven UX built for active traders/flippers, not portfolio holders. No buy/hold/sell synthesis — Intelligence Reports surfaces ratios but the user still has to interpret them into decisions. `[Certain]` — [[sources/marketmovers-features]]

**Watch closely**: Intelligence Reports is the feature most likely to expand toward decision synthesis if Market Movers sees SlabMetrics gain traction.
